description_long: This crustacean features a robust, oval-shaped abdomen with distinct dark brown bands running across a pale beige body. Its cephalothorax is compact and leads to two large, dark reddish-brown pincers that are prominently jointed and textured. The walking legs are slender, pale, and slightly curved, extending from the sides of the body and giving the creature a wide stance. The surface of the exoskeleton appears hard and slightly glossy, with subtle segmentation visible along the body and limbs. No antennae or eyes are clearly visible, suggesting a streamlined head region. The coloration and form suggest a species adapted for burrowing or hiding in urban crevices.
description: A banded, beige and brown crustacean with large reddish pincers and slender legs, featuring a hard, segmented exoskeleton. No visible antennae or eyes.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad pincers extended outward and the banded abdomen behind, with all legs visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the oval, banded abdomen and the pronounced curvature of the pincers and legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the rounded, banded abdomen and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the pincer and leg articulation with the banded body.
